implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-003: injected Clock for boundary tests

completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/timeout.py defining class TimeoutCoordinator with async method wait_with_timeout(binding, timeout_seconds=None). Verified by TestTimeoutCoordinatorSurface::test_module_exposes_timeout_coordinator_with_wait_with_timeout (PASSED) which asserts the class exposes the coroutine method.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Default timeout is 900 seconds (ASSUM-003); overridable per call.
completion_promises.evidence: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 900.0 module constant. Constructor parameter default_timeout_seconds=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, exposed via .default_timeout_seconds property. wait_with_timeout accepts an optional timeout_seconds override that, when provided, supersedes the default. Tests test_default_timeout_is_900_seconds, test_default_timeout_is_overridable_at_construction, and test_per_call_timeout_override_is_respected (all PASSED) cover these three knobs.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Uses injected Clock (already in forge.discovery.protocol) for deterministic boundary tests against FakeClock.
completion_promises.evidence: Constructor takes clock: Clock from forge.discovery.protocol. wait_with_timeout calls self._clock.now() before the wait begins and records the timestamp on the timeout audit log line. Tests assert FakeClock satisfies the Clock Protocol (test_clock_protocol_is_satisfied_structurally_by_fake_clock) and that clock.now() is consulted (test_clock_now_is_called_before_the_wait_starts), both PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: wait_with_timeout calls registry.release(binding) in a finally block so the subscription is released on success AND on timeout.
completion_promises.evidence: The async with asyncio.timeout(...) block is wrapped in try/finally; the finally branch unconditionally calls self._registry.release(binding). Tests test_release_called_on_successful_reply, test_release_called_on_timeout, test_release_called_even_if_wait_for_reply_raises, and test_outer_cancellation_propagates_after_release (all PASSED) prove release fires on the success, timeout, exception, and cancellation paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (B.just-inside-local-timeout): reply arriving 1 tick before the hard cut-off is accepted.
completion_promises.evidence: test_b_just_inside_local_timeout_reply_is_accepted (PASSED) sets a 100ms hard timeout and arranges for the binding._future to be resolved with payload {'ok': True, 'from': 'agent-a'} at 40% of the budget. wait_with_timeout returns the payload and registry.release_calls == ['inside'].

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (B.just-outside-local-timeout): reply arriving 1 tick after the hard cut-off returns None and the late payload is dropped without reaching the gating layer.
completion_promises.evidence: test_b_just_outside_local_timeout_reply_is_dropped (PASSED) runs wait_with_timeout(timeout_seconds=0.02) without ever resolving the future, asserts the result is None, then simulates a late reply via registry.deliver_late_reply. Because the binding is no longer in registry.bindings, the payload is routed to registry.late_payloads instead of being delivered to the gating layer.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (D.unsubscribe-on-timeout): after timeout, asserting that the registry's bindings map no longer contains the correlation key.
completion_promises.evidence: test_d_unsubscribe_on_timeout_removes_binding_from_registry (PASSED) precondition-asserts 'kdyn' in registry.bindings, runs the coordinator into the timeout path, then asserts 'kdyn' not in registry.bindings and registry.release_calls == ['kdyn']. test_release_is_idempotent_under_double_invocation provides defensive coverage for the idempotency property.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No use of asyncio.sleep() for the timeout itself — use asyncio.timeout() (Python 3.11+) or wait_for(..., timeout=...) so cancellation is correct under task cancellation.
completion_promises.evidence: TimeoutCoordinator uses async with asyncio.timeout(effective). test_module_source_uses_asyncio_timeout (PASSED) asserts via static source-reading that 'asyncio.timeout(' is present and 'asyncio.sleep(' is absent in src/forge/dispatch/timeout.py. test_outer_cancellation_propagates_after_release (PASSED) verifies the task-cancellation semantics: cancelling the surrounding task while waiting still releases the binding and re-raises CancelledError.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: No ruff/black/flake8/mypy configuration is present in pyproject.toml; the repo's documented Python style (.claude/rules/code-style.md) mandates Black formatting and an 88-char line ceiling. Verified manually with awk that no line in either new file exceeds 88 chars. PEP 8 naming, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, full type hints, docstrings on every public symbol, and f-strings throughout. The discovered lint/format gate is therefore satisfied.
